non_blocking_findings: Full installed-extension plus live model page E2E still needs user-side confirmation after reloading the unpacked legacy extension.
Manual refusal detection is conservative text parsing. If a model describes path/content in an unsupported format, the panel may not appear and the user can still use the normal MCP tool flow.
Permission store read/modify/write is not atomic; high-concurrency approval traffic could lose updates.
Windows path normalization does not fully account for junctions, symlinks, UNC long paths, or 8.3 short names.
One-time token consumption happens before the final filesystem write; if the write fails afterward, the user must request a new approval.

notes: The provided resume id f9aa195b-a403-42e5-ad5d-d89ee16d05be was not found in this CLI context.
Two strict full-context Claude review attempts timed out and were cleaned up.
A bounded key-path review prompt completed and returned NO_ISSUES_FOUND for backend permission bypass, marker approve/retry preservation, and UI recursion risk.
